codeReview.summary: The feature's primary goal was deploying the worldlabs-proxy edge function and creating the worldview-takes storage bucket. Actual deployment was blocked by the absence of SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN in the environment. The worker correctly identified this blocker, verified all pre-existing code (edge function, nano-banana routing, fal-proxy model handling), and produced comprehensive deployment documentation (docs/worldview-deployment.md, 114 lines) covering: deployment commands, secret configuration, storage bucket creation (3 methods), fal-proxy model verification, and error handling reference table. The feature description anticipated documentation as an acceptable fallback ('Note: If the Supabase CLI can't create buckets directly, document the manual step needed'). All automated checks pass (typecheck clean, 44 worldview tests passing, no new lint errors). The edge function code itself was created and tested in an earlier feature (commit 5d8e7cd) and is well-structured with proper auth, CORS, error handling, and action routing.

issues.description: The Supabase project ref 'ixkkrousepsiorwlaycp' is hardcoded in multiple places (line 3 prerequisites, line 37 curl test, line 54 dashboard URL, line 68 API URL). While this is documentation rather than runtime code, it could be a concern if the repo becomes public. Consider using a placeholder like '<project-ref>' or referencing supabase/config.toml.

Option C (Management API) for bucket creation uses the Authorization header with service-role-key in a curl command. While it says '<service-role-key>' as a placeholder, someone could accidentally paste a real key into the docs. The Dashboard UI option is safer to recommend as primary.

The deployment doc doesn't mention setting up RLS (Row-Level Security) policies for the worldview-takes storage bucket. If the bucket is public, any authenticated user can upload. Depending on requirements, storage policies may be needed to restrict uploads to the bucket owner.

sharedStateObservations.observation: services.yaml already includes 'deploy-worldlabs-proxy: npx supabase functions deploy worldlabs-proxy' — this is correct and was presumably added by a prior worker or the orchestrator. Good that it's documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml contains 'deploy-worldlabs-proxy: npx supabase functions deploy worldlabs-proxy'

sharedStateObservations.observation: SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN is required for deployment but not documented in environment.md or AGENTS.md. The worker identified this gap in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges but didn't add it. environment.md documents edge function env vars (WORLDLABS_API_KEY, FAL_KEY) but not the CLI deployment prerequisite.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker transcript shows deployment failed with 'Access token not provided'. environment.md at .factory/library/environment.md lists WORLDLABS_API_KEY but not SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN. Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges mentions: 'The worldview-worker skill could include a note about deployment features requiring SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN'.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worldview-worker skill (SKILL.md) is implementation-focused — it covers types, stores, services, edge functions, and UI components. It has no guidance for operational/deployment tasks. The worker followed the skill procedure (followedProcedure: true) but the skill didn't help with deployment-specific concerns like Supabase CLI auth, bucket creation limitations, or verifying remote deployments.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/worldview-worker/SKILL.md — 'Work Procedure' covers code implementation (understand, examine, test, implement, verify, commit) but has no section on deployment or operational tasks. Worker spent time discovering CLI auth requirements and Docker limitations.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The git repository root is /Users/gratitud3 (the home directory), not the project directory. This means all commits show paths prefixed with 'Downloads/mogstudio-factory/'. This is a pre-existing repo configuration issue that affects all workers.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git rev-parse --show-toplevel returns /Users/gratitud3. Commit 0c020f1 shows path: Downloads/mogstudio-factory/docs/worldview-deployment.md

summary: PASS. The worldview-deployment feature produced comprehensive deployment documentation (docs/worldview-deployment.md) covering edge function deployment, storage bucket creation, and model routing verification. Actual deployment was blocked by the absence of SUPABASE_ACCESS_TOKEN — an environmental limitation the worker correctly identified and documented. All pre-existing code was verified: worldlabs-proxy edge function is well-structured with proper auth/CORS/error handling, nano-banana correctly routes to fal-ai/flux/dev in worldLabsService.ts, and fal-proxy handles all Worldview models via dynamic endpoint routing. All automated checks pass. Three non-blocking issues noted: hardcoded project ref in docs, no RLS policy mention for storage bucket, and service-role-key placeholder in curl examples.
